metaDescription: How to link a Stripe account to your Webflow e-commerce store so checkout pages can process payments securely.
intro: Turning a Webflow design into a functioning online store means connecting a payment processor, and Stripe is one of the most common choices. This guide walks through enabling e-commerce settings, authorizing the Stripe connection, and testing checkout before going live.

steps.heading: Open your E-commerce settings
steps.body: Click the shopping cart icon in the left sidebar, then click Settings to reach the store setup panel.

steps.heading: Start the Stripe connection
steps.body: Scroll to the Payment Gateway section and click Connect next to the Stripe option.

steps.heading: Authorize your account
steps.body: Complete the secure Stripe Connect authorization overlay, entering your merchant credentials and two-factor verification.

steps.heading: Select your business profile
steps.body: Choose the correct business profile inside Stripe and click Connect to link the two platforms.

steps.heading: Verify and test
steps.body: Confirm Webflow shows a green Connected status, customize your checkout form fields, then use Test Mode with sandbox card details before publishing your store live.

faq.question: Is my Stripe login information stored inside Webflow?
faq.answer: No, the connection happens through Stripe's own secure authorization overlay rather than storing your credentials in Webflow.

faq.question: How do I test checkout before real customers use it?
faq.answer: Activate Test Mode and process a mock purchase using Stripe's sandbox card credentials to check how the system responds.

faq.question: How do I know the connection succeeded?
faq.answer: Return to your Webflow e-commerce settings and look for a green Connected status indicator next to the Stripe option.

faq.question: Can I customize the checkout page design?
faq.answer: Yes, the checkout page layout is a standard Webflow asset you can arrange and style like any other page element.
